danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player and NPCs; forces time-pressure decisions
Environmental danger requiring navigation skill; instant failure state
Syndicate distraction tactic; city under active threat from criminal organization

danger_signals.signal: Enemy weapon pickup and cooperative attacks
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat level; enemies are intelligent and adaptive

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual/narrative twist on familiar NPC appearance
danger_signals.meaning: Institutional corruption; inability to trust authority figures by sight alone

safety_signals.meaning: Survival mechanic; restore player vitality during combat sections
Player empowerment; no health cost for powered abilities at full meter
Alternative combat resource that bypasses weapon durability limitation

safety_signals.signal: Alternate stage routes contingent on enemy clearance
safety_signals.visual_form: Dynamic level design based on player performance
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency; successful combat performance unlocks safer/alternative paths

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: RoboCy Corporation branding as cover organization
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Robot duplicates of city officials; generic syndicate thugs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bomb placements throughout Wood Oak City; research facility in forest hideout

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Police authority connection through characters Axel, Blaze, Adam Hunter
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: City hall; police institutional spaces; Dr. Zan's association with exposing corruption
City infrastructure; official spaces targeted for replacement

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Early game: Syndicate active, officials being replaced
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bombs placed around city; RoboCy Corporation operations visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy forces actively hunting and replacing officials; NPCs in danger from poisonous gas traps

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Mid-game: Task force progressing through city levels
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alternate stage routes based on player success clearing enemy waves
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies escalate tactics with weapon pickup, blocking, cooperative attacks, health stealing

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Late game: Confrontation at Mr. X's robot factory
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Forest hideout as final stage location; laboratory setting reveals brain-controlled command center
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Robot Y final boss; Mr. X brain capsule as control node; self-destruct sequence triggered on defeat

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-game resolution (true ending): Mr. X eliminated, Ivan rescued
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Restored city authority; RoboCy becomes defunct; Dr. Dahm neutralized
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Adam rescues heroes from self-destruct; institutional order restored; Dr. Zan's name cleared

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Four possible ending states depending on difficulty setting and stage completion times
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Variable narrative outcomes based on player performance metrics

environmental_storytelling.example: Robot duplicates of city officials visually indistinguishable; narrative reveals institutional corruption vulnerability
Poisonous gas in enclosed rooms creates timed rescue scenarios for NPCs; forces player choice between clearing enemies and saving lives

environmental_storytelling.technique: Stage design reflecting criminal organization strategy
environmental_storytelling.example: Bomb placements throughout city stages visualize Syndicate's distraction tactic while core operation occurs at research facility
Dr. Zan's presence and weapon-to-energy conversion reflects technological arms race between player task force and RoboCy roboticist program
Stage paths unlock based on enemy clearance success, visualizing player agency impact on NPC survival and city security

environmental_storytelling.technique: Final location architecture reveals true threat
environmental_storytelling.example: Robot factory in forest hideout transitions to laboratory interior revealing Mr. X as disembodied brain; visual design communicates scale of criminal infrastructure

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Pick up weapons, block attacks, employ cooperative tactics, steal exposed food items to regain health
Visually identical to replaced officials; controlled remotely by Mr. X's brain-centered command network
Require player to clear enemy room before gas exposure kills them; creates time-pressure decision making
Each character has respective strengths/weaknesses; participate in task force coordination
Automatically converts all picked-up weapons into energy balls; cannot hold weapons; reflects whistleblower/technological specialist role
Unable to hold weapons; reward player skill progression and conditional stage completion
Disembodied brain in laboratory capsule; controls all robot forces telepathically; initiates self-destruct sequence on imminent defeat

notes: SoR3 communicates world-state primarily through plot mechanics and environmental hazards rather than visual aesthetic shifts. Faction identity is narrative-driven (Syndicate vs. Police task force) with limited visual faction branding. Danger signals manifest as environmental design choices (gas, pits, bombs) and enemy behavior escalation. Political situation is conveyed through robot replacement plot device, which creates visual ambiguity about NPC trustworthiness. Alternate stage routes and multiple endings create branching world-state visualization based on player performance. The game's manifestation layer relies on stage design, NPC behavior, and enemy AI adaptation rather than color-coded faction systems or environmental mood shifts.
